artPrompt: A single calendar day torn from exile and dragged back into the week, a luminous square of golden afternoon light hauled upward by a rope of green wash, ivory negative space all around like missing days. Small townsfolk silhouettes pull from the lower edge in a tug-of-war of gesture. A dramatic civic reinstatement caught mid-pull, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Recall Target Tuesday (the rival took our Tuesday and we want it back)
text:
Somewhere at this table, a rival exiled a Tuesday. Recall it. Hold a special election to reinstate Tuesday to its former office in the week.
Every day votes. Monday abstains, as is its custom. Wednesday filibusters. The Tuesday is reinstated, slightly haunted, and now owes the movement a favor.
Draw a conclusion, then draw a card, then draw the card's art from memory and feel watched.
flavorText: You cannot have a recall without something to recall. The rival deck obligingly removed a day. We organized.

extras.interaction: directly answers the rival's Exile Target Tuesday
